operator_scope.semantic_signature: candidate tiled schedule for KBX permute_copy or regular materialized layout transform after correctness passes

valid_when: Task is KBX permute_copy or a regular materialized transpose/permute/strided copy and a stride-correct candidate is hidden-correct.
Profile summary indicates memory-bound or uncoalesced global-memory access.
invalid_when: Dominant operation is arbitrary-index gather/scatter, masked_select compaction, or pointwise where rather than regular affine layout transform.
Correctness for edge tiles and non-contiguous strides has not passed.

mechanism: Regular layout transforms can use tile schedules to make one or both global-memory legs more coalesced, but shared-memory tiles can add bank conflicts, synchronization, and occupancy pressure.

instructions: Use a 2D or ND tile schedule that maps source and destination logical coordinates explicitly and keeps edge masks for partial tiles.
Benchmark coalesced-read and coalesced-write variants when cheap; choose based on repeated p50/p95, not intuition.
Consider a shared-memory tile only after direct stride-aware copy is correct and profile attributes the bottleneck to uncoalesced regular access.
Do not call torch.permute, torch.transpose, contiguous(), reshape(), or clone() to perform the target copy in the submitted path.

expected_effect.correctness: No effect if the stride semantic capsule is already followed.
expected_effect.performance: Candidate-only: may improve regular layout-copy coalescing; no H20 speedup is asserted.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Bounds schedule search for KBX permute_copy after correctness.
expected_effect.risk: Wrong coordinate mapping, bank conflicts, or synchronization overhead can erase gains.

correctness_matrix: small dimension less than tile size
performance_requirements: Compare direct stride-aware copy, coalesced-read tile, and coalesced-write tile under identical protocol before promotion.
validation.promotion_policy: Requires H20 benchmark/profile evidence and preferably an ablation against direct copy.

known_failures: Tiling with conflated source/destination strides gives wrong coordinates.
Partial tiles without masks can read/write out of bounds.
Shared-memory tiles can introduce bank conflicts or reduce occupancy.
